title: DORA Outsourcing Registers — Function Mapping Completeness for Each Outsourced Arrangement

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'unspecified' → 'Art.5'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
| inputlookup dora_outsourcing_register.csv
| eval fc_len=len(function_codes)
| eval validated=strptime(last_validated,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where fc_len<5 OR isnull(function_codes) OR validated<relative_time(now(),"-365d@d")
| table contract_id, provider_id, function_codes, last_validated
value: Registers must reflect which functions depend on each provider. Flagging contracts without mapped internal business functions prevents hollow register entries that fail supervisory scrutiny during inspections.
implementation: (1) Define allowed `function_codes` taxonomy; (2) workflow assigns business owner per contract; (3) integrate procurement go-live events; (4) quarterly validation campaign; (5) tie to BIA criticality.
visualization: Table (incomplete), Donut (validation age), Single value (contracts to fix).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
